title: Altered crosstalk of bacterial lipopolysaccharide with immune cells in colorectal cancer compared to paired adjacent intestinal tissue.
snippet: Commensal bacteria play a crucial role in modulating human immune responses in the intestine. Under homeostatic conditions, the gut microbiota is tightly regulated by interactions with the mucosal immune system. However, colorectal cancer (CRC) is characterized by an imbalance in bacterial composition and bacterial translocation across the intestinal barrie...
